summary: The four targeted code fixes are mostly correct, but the broadened PEM rule introduces a concrete false positive in the diff: the new Codex review JSON contains the literal encrypted-PEM example without an allowlist marker, and the detector now flags that file as a private key.

findings.title: Broadened PEM rule flags review artifact
findings.claim: This newly added review artifact contains the literal example `-----BEGIN ENCRYPTED PRIVATE KEY-----` without the allowlist marker. With the broadened `PEM_RE`, `findSecrets` reports this line as `private-key`, so a squash/application of this diff or any future edit that stages this file will be blocked even though the content is documentation of a synthetic example, not a secret.
findings.suggestion: Sanitize the PEM example in this JSON artifact, split/escape it the way the tests do, or add the `pragma: allowlist secret` marker on the same line if keeping the literal example is intentional.
